title: Frontend UX Polish
description: Improve UI readability, hierarchy, motion, and responsiveness without changing product intent.

input_schema.ui_description: string — current layout, user pain points, responsive issues, hierarchy problems (e.g. 'Card-based dashboard; cards stack vertically on mobile but horizontal scroll on 768-1024px tablets; primary metrics buried among secondary cards')
input_schema.constraints: string — WCAG level, brand palette, CSS framework, max animation duration, supported browsers (e.g. 'WCAG AA, Material 3 palette, Tailwind CSS, 200ms max animation, Chrome 90+, iOS 13+')

instructions: Audit typography, spacing rhythm, and visual hierarchy.
Suggest a coherent color and contrast strategy that meets the specified WCAG level.
Add meaningful motion only for state transitions — not decorative. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
Ensure layout quality on both mobile and desktop breakpoints.
Verify contrast ratios meet target accessibility standard.

examples.input: Card-based dashboard; cards stack on mobile but horizontal scroll on 768-1024px; primary metrics buried. Constraints: WCAG AA, Tailwind, 200ms max animation.
examples.output: Typography: bump heading from 1.25rem to 1.5rem, body stays 1rem (contrast ratio 4.8:1 verified). Spacing: adopt 8px grid, cards get 16px gap. Layout: <=768px single column, 769-1024px 2-col grid (no scroll), >=1025px 3-col. Motion: 150ms fade-in for primary metric cards on mount (state transition), respect prefers-reduced-motion. Hierarchy: primary metrics pinned top row with accent border-left.
